children.name: RegisterPatient
children.purpose: Register a new patient in the system
children.behavior: Extract patient details from input, validate uniqueness, store in patients data store, and return success or error
postconditions: patient record created in patients data store

inputs.name: patient_data
inputs.description: Patient registration details (name, age, contact, etc.)

outputs.name: registration_result
outputs.description: Result with success status, patient ID, and message

global_vars.description: Write new patient record to patients data store

constraints.description: patient_data must contain all required fields (name, age, contact)
patient ID must be unique across all patients

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid patient_data, a new patient record is created and a unique patient ID is returned
Given duplicate patient_data, an error is returned and no record is created

children.name: BookAppointment
children.purpose: Book an appointment for a registered patient
children.behavior: Extract patient ID and appointment details from input, verify patient exists, check slot availability, store appointment in appointments data store, and return confirmation
postconditions: appointment record created in appointments data store

inputs.name: appointment_data
inputs.description: Appointment details (patient_id, date, time, doctor, etc.)

outputs.name: booking_result
outputs.description: Result with success status, appointment ID, and message

global_vars.description: Read patient record to verify existence

constraints.description: appointment_data must contain valid patient_id, date, time, and doctor
appointment slot must not overlap with existing appointments for the same doctor

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid appointment_data for an existing patient, a new appointment is created and an appointment ID is returned
Given appointment_data for a non-existent patient, an error is returned
Given appointment_data for an already booked slot, an error is returned

children.name: GetPatientRecords
children.purpose: Retrieve medical records for a patient
children.behavior: Extract patient ID from input, verify patient exists, read records from patients data store (append-only), and return records

outputs.name: records_result
outputs.description: Result with success status, records list, and message

global_vars.description: Read patient record and medical records

constraints.description: patient_id must be a valid string
records are append-only and cannot be modified

acceptance_criteria.description: Given a valid patient_id for an existing patient, the patient's records are returned
Given a patient_id for a non-existent patient, an error is returned

children.name: UpdatePatientProfile
children.behavior: Extract patient ID and updated fields from input, verify patient exists, update profile in patients data store, and return success
postconditions: patient profile updated in patients data store

inputs.name: update_data
inputs.description: Patient ID and updated profile fields

outputs.name: update_result
outputs.description: Result with success status and message

global_vars.description: Read patient record to verify existence, then write updated profile

constraints.description: update_data must contain valid patient_id and at least one field to update
Only allowed profile fields can be updated (name, age, contact)

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid update_data for an existing patient, the profile is updated and success is returned
Given update_data for a non-existent patient, an error is returned
Given update_data with no fields to update, an error is returned

governance_notes: All children operate on the patients data store (read/write as specified). BookAppointment additionally uses appointments data store. GetPatientRecords is a pure read-only function. RegisterPatient, BookAppointment, and UpdatePatientProfile are atomic operations requiring write capabilities. Constraints ensure data integrity and validation. Acceptance criteria cover success and failure cases. Traceability links each child to a parent functional requirement.
